headline: ROTATION CONFIRMATION (first agent assessment) WITH NEGATIVE-PRESSURE FLAG. Live composite 62.2 (Established). Note: scanner rotation_state listed 73.3; using live data per assessor protocol — DATA INTEGRITY FLAG. In-window evidence: April 16 execution of Omar Bin Yacob Bamadhaj for marijuana trafficking (1009.1g); 8 drug-related executions in first months of 2026 (vs 17 total in 2025, 15 of which drug-related); OHCHR April 2026 statement: UN Human Rights Chief Türk 'alarmed by increase in drug-related executions, calls for moratorium' — sustained negative ACC/EQU/BND signal. Migrant workers excluded from Employment Act, tied-visa system, restrictions on labor organizing — sustained EQU concern. Composite 62.2 (Established mid-band) appropriately reflects strong governance/economic-rights performance against persistent death-penalty and migrant-worker concerns. No 5pt-threshold-crossing acute event. Composite holds.
data_integrity_flag: Scanner rotation_state listed Singapore at 73.3; live data is 62.2. Used live per assessor protocol.

evidence.finding: UN Human Rights Chief Türk 'alarmed by increase in drug-related executions, calls for moratorium'. April 16 hanging of Omar Bin Yacob Bamadhaj; 8 drug-related executions in first months of 2026.
Migrant workers excluded from Employment Act; tied-visa system gives employers unilateral termination/repatriation power; labor organizing restricted.
Universal Periodic Review submission documents systematic concerns including death penalty, migrant rights, civil liberties.

negativeSignals: April 2026 OHCHR moratorium call following 8 drug-related executions; tied-visa migrant-worker system; death penalty regime persistence.
positiveSignals: Strong governance, economic rights, public services performance reflected in 4.0 ACT and SYS subscores.
